meta.label: relation
meta.content: A first-class, reified relationship between two entities. Use a `relation` whenever the link itself carries time, strength, provenance, or any field of its own (ownership, employment, kinship, derivation). Inline a structural link as a property when none of those apply (e.g. `region.country = 'Bahamas'`).

subject.description: Slug / id of the source entity

predicate.description: Verb: owns | employs | located_in | part_of | child_of | derived_from | originated_in | produces | consumes | participates_in | inhabits | mentions | ...

object.description: Slug / id of the target entity

context.description: Optional scope where the relation holds (an org, a project, a jurisdiction)

_provenance.rationale: Subsumes ownership, role, origin, provenance — all of which were thin components describing one kind of relationship.
